title: Rich Indian Paneer Curry
description: A creamy and aromatic lacto-vegetarian curry featuring marinated paneer, fennel seeds, and a hint of mustard, perfect for a date night dinner.
instructions: Marinate the paneer cubes in a mixture of yogurt, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt for at least 30 minutes.
Heat the ghee or oil in a large pan over medium heat and add the fennel seeds.
Let the seeds sizzle for a few seconds, then add the cumin, coriander, turmeric, and chili powder.
Stir the spices for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the marinated paneer to the pan and cook until it is lightly browned on all sides.
Reduce the heat to low and add 1 cup of water to the pan, stirring to scrape up any browned bits.
Let the curry simmer for 10-15 minutes, until the sauce has thickened and the paneer is fully coated.
Season the curry with salt to taste, then garnish with chopped cilantro.
Serve the curry hot over basmati rice or with naan bread.
